objective: Treat `ImplementationPlans/aether-flow-documents-navigation-implementation-plan.md` as accepted implementation authority. Normalize and validate it without strengthening scientific claims or external-effect permissions, then execute it as a serial, dependency-aware relay. Each launcher generation may select and dispatch exactly one immutable dependency-ready task to exactly one fresh worker discussion and must execute zero AgentJobs itself. Each worker may execute at most one AgentJob. Reuse the current checkout. Do not create a branch or worktree, commit, push, deploy, mutate upstream sources, or publish new scientific claims without separate explicit authorization.

acceptance_criteria: The plan-control validator is revision-bound and the document model, import ownership, and publication safety boundaries are implemented without exposing unapproved source material.
phases.title: Establish the document foundation

acceptance_criteria: The canonical Documents overview, category pages, and retained contextual routes exist with source-role and non-authority boundaries preserved.
phases.title: Create the canonical Documents route family

acceptance_criteria: Documents replaces Resources in primary navigation and every retired route has a direct semantic destination without loops or avoidable chains.
phases.title: Replace navigation and migrate routes

acceptance_criteria: Canonical route, provenance, manifest, maintainer-documentation, and cleanup records describe the final source state.
phases.title: Finalize manifests, provenance, documentation, and cleanup

acceptance_criteria: The bounded build, interaction, redirect, manifest, provenance, Cloudflare, and aggregate checks are recorded while external release remains separate.
phases.title: Verify and record release readiness

acceptance_criteria: Plan-goal validation accepts the current explicitly bound starting revision instead of a hard-coded historical installation revision.
Authorized active or terminal plan changes are accepted only when their paths and lifecycle evidence match the exact canonical binding and receipts.
Unrelated reader-facing drift, forbidden mutable paths, topology changes, open intents, leases, and external effects remain blocking.
Focused regression tests distinguish accepted plan-controlled changes from unrelated drift.
tasks.objective: Replace stale installation-baseline assumptions in the website plan-goal validator with checks against the accepted live repository binding and exact plan-controlled changes, without weakening installed-bundle, topology, drift, or external-effect safeguards.
tasks.title: Make plan-goal validation revision-bound

acceptance_criteria: The live starting revision and its divergence from the inspected baseline are recorded in direct task evidence.
The ontology importer replaces only ontology-owned records and preserves unrelated PDF and TeX rows.
Focused regression evidence covers preservation without changing existing ontology asset URLs.
tasks.objective: Re-check the live main checkout against the accepted baseline and restrict ontology import ownership so unrelated PDF and TeX manifest records are preserved.
tasks.title: Establish the live baseline and safe ontology ownership
validation_refs: manual inspection of targeted pytest results for ontology importer ownership and unrelated PDF and TeX preservation

acceptance_criteria: Markdown is represented as an explicit validated manifest kind with a public URL and source identity.
Manifest validation rejects malformed or unapproved Markdown records.
No recursive Markdown publication or private local path exposure is introduced.
tasks.objective: Add the bounded Markdown manifest kind and validation needed for approved governance documents without making arbitrary repository Markdown public.
tasks.title: Add first-class Markdown manifest support

acceptance_criteria: One logical document can group multiple format manifestations without flattening their authority roles.
The document catalog has deterministic validation and does not duplicate source or asset manifest authority.
The model remains static, local, and free of a new runtime dependency or service.
tasks.objective: Define small generic document-category, logical-document, format, status, and authority-role types plus a validated document catalog.
tasks.title: Create the document domain model and catalog

acceptance_criteria: Collection imports declare explicit ownership and preserve records owned by other importers.
Imported records bind public bytes, hashes, source references, logical-document identity, and format role.
Ambiguous ownership or source status fails closed without publishing files.
tasks.objective: Implement a small deterministic collection importer that updates only its owned catalog and manifest records.
tasks.title: Add the collection-oriented document importer
validation_refs: manual inspection of targeted collection importer preservation and deterministic hash results

acceptance_criteria: Only explicitly allowlisted approved governance Markdown can enter the public catalog.
Private paths, credentials, local control records, and unreviewed current-state material fail closed.
Markdown response headers are explicit and do not weaken existing security policy.
tasks.objective: Require explicit governance Markdown allowlisting, bounded deny-pattern checks, and safe public response headers before any such asset can publish.
tasks.title: Enforce governance publication safety and Markdown headers
validation_refs: manual inspection of governance Markdown allowlist and deny-pattern boundaries

acceptance_criteria: The page identity is Documentation Overview rather than Library or Resources.
The page explains all five categories and preserves format-specific authority and provenance boundaries.
Primary reader journeys are internal-first and the page contains no dedicated Source authority section.
tasks.objective: Create `/documents/` as the canonical Documentation Overview with categories, formats, authority roles, provenance, status, and reading paths.
tasks.title: Build Documentation Overview
validation_refs: manual inspection of Documentation Overview source hierarchy links authority language and responsive layout

acceptance_criteria: Every visible anthology item has an approved catalog-backed PDF or the page clearly reports that no item is published.
Planned material is not represented as published and PDF role and provenance remain explicit.
The category remains useful without inventing or importing unapproved content.
tasks.objective: Create `/documents/anthology/` as a PDF-oriented collection that exposes only approved published anthology items and otherwise reports its bounded collection state.
tasks.title: Build Anthology Articles
validation_refs: manual inspection of Anthology Articles catalog filtering empty state and provenance language

acceptance_criteria: Action labels distinguish source inspection from readable derivatives and avoid generic unlabeled downloads.
Components preserve existing consumers or migrate them within this exact boundary.
Metadata wraps safely and no broad visual-system redesign or dependency is introduced.
tasks.objective: Generalize document actions and add only the small reusable collection, card, action, and metadata components required by the Documents pages.
tasks.title: Generalize document presentation components
validation_refs: manual inspection of document component APIs existing consumers action labels focus behavior and metadata wrapping

acceptance_criteria: Existing ontology articles remain available as logical TeX and PDF pairs in their canonical reading order.
Registered TeX and readable PDF roles remain distinct and existing physical asset URLs do not move.
The page is a Research Articles collection rather than the general Documents overview.
tasks.objective: Create `/documents/research/` from the ontology TeX and PDF collection while preserving sequence, stable asset URLs, and distinct source and derivative roles.
tasks.title: Build Research Articles
validation_refs: manual inspection of Research Articles ontology order logical grouping source and derivative labels and asset URLs

acceptance_criteria: Governance entries expose scope, status, freshness, type, and provenance without presenting unapproved Markdown.
The category routes readers to retained contextual pages and keeps repository layout secondary.
No governance artifact is promoted beyond its registered status.
tasks.objective: Create `/documents/governance/` as the category front door for approved governance, control, registry, publication, and workflow material.
tasks.title: Build Governance & Control
validation_refs: manual inspection of Governance & Control scope status freshness provenance and allowlist behavior

acceptance_criteria: The complete approved diagram inventory and conceptual grouping are preserved without duplicates.
The existing gallery component contract, accessibility metadata, provenance, and non-authority labels remain intact.
No SVG policy exception or visible embedded SVG text is introduced.
tasks.objective: Create `/documents/diagrams/` by relocating the gallery page while preserving manifest filtering, conceptual groups, captions, alt text, and non-authority framing.
tasks.title: Move and preserve Diagram Gallery
validation_refs: manual inspection of Diagram Gallery inventory captions alt text provenance and non-authority framing

acceptance_criteria: Both canonical governance routes exist and preserve their existing reviewed claim boundaries.
Breadcrumbs, metadata, and contextual links use the Documents architecture.
The category pages do not render a dedicated Source authority section.
tasks.objective: Create the Documents governance Source Authority and Registries routes from their existing reader-facing content without strengthening authority claims.
tasks.title: Relocate Source Authority and Registries
validation_refs: manual inspection of relocated Source Authority and Registries route identity links and claim parity

acceptance_criteria: Both canonical governance routes exist with reviewed content and internal-first related links.
Publication workflow and retrieval-layer explanations retain their source and non-authority qualifications.
No workflow evidence is presented as scientific proof.
tasks.objective: Create the Documents governance Publication Process and Retrieval Layers routes while preserving operational and provenance boundaries.
tasks.title: Relocate Publication Process and Retrieval Layers
validation_refs: manual inspection of relocated Publication Process and Retrieval Layers route identity links and qualifications

acceptance_criteria: Both canonical governance routes exist and are contextual rather than primary dropdown destinations.
Repository and builder guidance is current for the Documents architecture.
Reader journeys remain internal-first with GitHub retained for provenance.
tasks.objective: Create the Documents governance Repository Map and Site Builder Guide routes as secondary maintainer-oriented reader destinations.
tasks.title: Relocate Repository Map and Site Builder Guide
validation_refs: manual inspection of relocated Repository Map and Site Builder Guide route identity links and maintainer framing

acceptance_criteria: The canonical reading-path index and general-public route exist with no duplicate guided-start identity.
Reading paths use canonical Documents category routes and preserve audience-specific guidance.
No retired Resources route is required for the primary journey.
tasks.objective: Create canonical Documents reading-path routes and consolidate the existing guided starts without losing the general-public path.
tasks.title: Consolidate Reading Paths and Guided Starts
validation_refs: manual inspection of reading path consolidation audience routing canonical links and duplicate removal

acceptance_criteria: Both canonical routes exist and remain contextual destinations outside the five-item dropdown.
Reviewer evidence and generated derivatives are not promoted beyond their recorded status.
Links, breadcrumbs, metadata, and provenance use canonical Documents routes.
tasks.objective: Create the canonical Documents Reviewer Packet and Derivatives routes with their existing evidence and generated-output boundaries preserved.
tasks.title: Relocate Reviewer Packet and Generated Derivatives
validation_refs: manual inspection of relocated Reviewer Packet and Derivatives route identity links evidence boundaries and status labels

acceptance_criteria: The dropdown label is Documents and its entries are exactly Documentation Overview, Anthology Articles, Research Articles, Governance & Control, and Diagram Gallery in that order.
Parent active state covers nested Documents routes and every child link is internal-first.
Desktop, mobile, keyboard, no-script, Escape, outside-click, and focus-return behavior remains unchanged unless a directly evidenced defect requires a bounded fix.
tasks.objective: Replace the top-level Resources navigation object with Documents and exactly five ordered category-level entries while preserving header interaction semantics.
tasks.title: Replace Resources navigation with Documents
validation_refs: manual inspection of Documents navigation order active-state prefixes mobile disclosure keyboard behavior and no-script parity

acceptance_criteria: Primary internal journeys point directly to canonical Documents routes rather than relying on redirects.
Library and Resources product identities are removed while legitimate generic prose remains.
No GitHub provenance link is removed merely because an internal reader route exists.
tasks.objective: Inventory and replace primary internal Resources links and stale product labels while retaining legitimate generic uses of the word resources.
tasks.title: Update internal links and public labels

acceptance_criteria: `/documents` normalizes to `/documents/` and no Documents route redirects into Resources.
Redirect ownership has one declared source of truth and no loop is present.
Cloudflare and Astro redirect behavior agree for the affected aliases.
tasks.objective: Remove redirects that point Documents or related aliases back into Resources before the new canonical route family is activated.
tasks.title: Replace reverse Documents redirects

acceptance_criteria: Every route in the accepted migration matrix has one semantic 301 destination.
No blanket Resources wildcard hides an unmapped route or creates duplicate canonical content.
Redirects are direct and do not depend on a second redirect.
tasks.objective: Add direct 301 mappings from every retired Resources route to its exact canonical Documents destination.
tasks.title: Add explicit semantic Resources redirects

acceptance_criteria: Legacy aliases resolve directly to final canonical routes without Resources intermediates.
Representative alias tests cover status and Location values.
No redirect chain or canonical contradiction remains.
tasks.objective: Repoint older aliases such as diagrams, equations, and downloads directly to their final Documents destinations.
tasks.title: Repoint legacy project aliases directly

acceptance_criteria: Retired Resources page sources no longer build duplicate canonical content.
Every removed page has a verified canonical destination and direct redirect.
The deletion does not remove shared components or unrelated resource terminology.
tasks.objective: Remove the old Resources page source family only after canonical pages, internal links, and explicit redirects are in place.
tasks.title: Remove retired Resources page sources

acceptance_criteria: All required Documents routes are represented and retired Resources routes are absent as canonical pages.
The route map reflects the final source paths and canonical URLs.
Required-route validation fails for a missing category route.
tasks.objective: Update required-route checks and the public route map to the final canonical Documents paths after page locations are stable.
tasks.title: Update required routes and route map

acceptance_criteria: Every canonical Documents page has matching source identity and page hash evidence.
Retired Resources sources are not represented as canonical provenance pages.
No private path or stale hash appears in public provenance.
tasks.objective: Regenerate page provenance only after canonical Documents page bytes and route-map locations are final.
tasks.title: Regenerate page provenance

acceptance_criteria: Catalog, source, and asset records cross-reference valid public bytes and hashes.
Unchanged ontology assets retain stable URLs and identities.
No manifest row claims scientific correctness or authority beyond its registered role.
tasks.objective: Finalize the document catalog and update source or asset manifests only for assets that actually changed or were lawfully added.
tasks.title: Finalize public document manifests

acceptance_criteria: README, AGENTS, documentation indexes, feature map, and Cloudflare deployment guidance use canonical Documents routes.
Source-authority, generated-derivative, validation, commit, push, and deployment boundaries remain explicit.
No instruction is weakened and the accepted plan is indexed for maintainers.
tasks.objective: Update repository, agent, feature-map, documentation-index, and deployment guidance for the canonical Documents architecture and separate release boundary.
tasks.title: Update maintainer documentation
validation_refs: manual inspection of maintainer documentation route examples authority boundaries validation guidance and deployment separation

acceptance_criteria: Dead product identifiers and route data are removed without deleting legitimate generic prose.
Document, ontology, and diagram references resolve to existing bytes with matching hashes.
No stale compatibility data, duplicate catalog entry, or unapproved asset move remains.
tasks.objective: Remove dead Resources and Library identifiers, prune dead route data, and confirm final document and diagram asset integrity without moving stable assets.
tasks.title: Remove compatibility names and confirm asset integrity

acceptance_criteria: The affected and required routes build as static Astro pages.
No build-time route collision, missing import, or broken asset reference remains.
The task records the exact build command and result without changing source.
tasks.objective: Run one final static Astro build against the completed canonical Documents source state and record direct output.
tasks.title: Run the final static build

acceptance_criteria: Dropdown order, active state, keyboard and focus behavior, compact disclosure, clipping, and overflow pass representative review.
Documentation Overview and every category route are reachable through the intended reader journey.
Observed browser-console or interaction defects are recorded rather than silently ignored.
tasks.objective: Perform one representative desktop and one compact headed-browser review of Documents navigation and the category landing pages.
tasks.title: Review desktop and compact Documents navigation
validation_refs: manual inspection of Documents navigation and category routes at representative desktop and compact viewports including keyboard focus overflow and browser console

acceptance_criteria: Representative old Resources and legacy aliases return direct 301 locations to their final Documents routes.
No loop, avoidable chain, or reverse Documents-to-Resources redirect is observed.
Any bounded redirect defect is repaired only within the declared redirect files.
tasks.objective: Validate Cloudflare configuration and representative canonical, Resources, and legacy redirect status and Location values.
tasks.title: Verify representative redirects
validation_refs: manual inspection of representative redirect status and Location values for canonical Resources and legacy aliases

acceptance_criteria: Manifest validation passes for all changed document records and assets.
Page provenance validation passes for final canonical route bytes.
Cloudflare configuration validation passes with the final headers and redirects.
tasks.objective: Run the change-relevant manifest, provenance, and Cloudflare validators once against the final bounded source state.
tasks.title: Run targeted manifest provenance and Cloudflare checks

acceptance_criteria: `npm run validate` passes against the final bounded implementation state.
Any pre-existing or newly introduced failure is distinguished with direct evidence.
A failing aggregate check does not authorize test weakening, unrelated cleanup, or external release.
tasks.objective: Run one aggregate repository validation against the final local source state and stop on any unresolved failure.
tasks.title: Run the aggregate repository validation

acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can reconstruct moved, consolidated, redirected, and retired routes and the checks actually run.
Commit, push, and deployment are recorded as not performed unless separately authorized and directly evidenced.
No release, upstream mutation, or scientific claim promotion is implied by implementation completion.
tasks.objective: Record the completed route, navigation, importer, manifest, provenance, and validation results plus intentionally skipped checks and external-effect status.
tasks.title: Record migration completion and release boundary
validation_refs: manual inspection of the Documents migration completion record changed routes validation evidence skipped checks and external-effect status

title: Documents Navigation and Documentation Architecture
